method_notes: E_PSYCHOLOGY_SPEC was opened only after the unit-by-unit unaided visual observations in E001 through E048 had been written and sealed.
The comparisons below reference those earlier records without changing or reinterpreting their stored text.
structural_observations: The file declares schema_version 1.0.0, identifies docs/art/unit_art_board_reference_criteria.md as its authority, and states that its purpose is Phase 2 ten-field survival-psychology and conditional-gate applicability records.
The units object contains exactly twelve keyed records: korath, veyra, cashmere, pilfer, nyxa, creep, knoll, quillith, kett, luna, malachor, and sable.

Every unit contains all ten named psychology fields with nonempty text: core_wound, private_want, primary_fear, survival_strategy, villainous_distortion, current_emotional_state, emotional_contradiction, face_direction, pose_direction, and forbidden_read.
Every unit also contains sex_design_category, age_gate, visual_lane, deliberately_horrific, conditional_gates, and exactly three nearest_neighbors.

The twelve nearest-neighbor arrays contain thirty-six references. Thirty-four resolve case-insensitively to unit keys in this file; the two Paisley references, under Cashmere and Luna, point outside this twelve-unit set.

All twelve conditional-gate maps contain hot_adult_woman, lean_toned_feminine, frontline_protection, and deliberate_horror_exception. Korath alone also contains blessed_special_gate, giving Korath five gate entries and every other unit four.
The conditional-gate key frontline_protection differs from the frontline_protection_explained verdict key recorded in the sealed E054 contract observation.

Pilfer, Nyxa, Malachor, and Sable use sex-design category tokens nonbinary, female_deliberate_horror_exception, sexless_grotesque_once_human, and nonbinary_androgynous. Those four tokens differ from the four accepted tokens recorded in the sealed E055 validator observation.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Failed public miracle; a need for suffering to be declared necessary and forgivable; fear that penance has no purpose; compulsory protection converted into moral debt; exhausted shame sharpened into accusation; service in conflict with resentment.
visual_correspondences: The sealed master and face reads independently identified an immense burden, split damaged anatomy, a bowed head, exhausted resentful vigilance, and an eye line that remains confrontational.
The broken overhead structure and unequal ruined body visibly correspond to continuing compulsory suffering, while the low open hand can accommodate an accusatory demand.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face read resolved most strongly as a familiar stern scowl and could not isolate accusation from grief, fatigue, controlled threat, or appeal.
The open palm read as beckoning or pleading as readily as demanding a debt.
The failed miracle, need for forgiveness, indispensability, and conversion of protection into moral obligation were not visually specific in the unaided evidence.
At 96 pixels, the face, hand action, chains, and split-material anatomy collapse, leaving the arch and giant mass as the dominant read.
preserved_uncertainties: Whether the pale side is exposed anatomy, fused armor, or another transformation remains unresolved by the images.
The exact target and intent of the open-hand gesture remain unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Trauma from an early breach; desire for a perfectly sealed world; fear of openings and independent motion; survival through nested enclosure; protection distorted into possession; possessive stillness around a watched larval core.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified nested pale plates, a compressed central opening, a bound inner body, a watchful upper slit, and static defensive enclosure.
Binding cords and layers physically support the themes of containment, sealing, and loss of freedom.
visual_discrepancies: Unaided inspection could not determine which aperture or inner structure is the controlling intelligence.
The central form could read as a victim, prey, offspring, or carried organism rather than a valued subject being possessively enclosed.
Fear of breach, annihilation of another's freedom, and the wish to stop independent motion were not independently legible as motives.
At 96 pixels, the form reads closer to a seed pod or environmental object; the bound core, teeth, and psychology disappear.
preserved_uncertainties: Predation, gestation, imprisonment, and symbiosis remain competing visual explanations for the shell-core relationship.
The movement and attack method remain visually uncertain.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: A paid kill erased a beloved face; desire to own people, knowledge, and outcomes; fear of unpriced uncertainty and missing memory; survival by pricing and contracting; people treated as assets; proprietary warmth covering concealed panic.
visual_correspondences: The sealed master read identified controlled authority, a large inscribed book, direct appraisal, and a precise forward hand, which can visually support classification, ownership, or contract activity.
Mature composure and steady eye contact correspond to the specified pleasurable certainty.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face read found near-symmetrical beauty-model seriousness, softly closed lips, and no decisive visible want, fear, compulsion, or emotional contradiction.
The specified split between one sharply appraising eye and one fractionally unfocused eye, tiny brow pinch, tight jaw, and inviting mouth corner did not register in the unaided face record.
The book could be an occult tome rather than a ledger, and the blackened hand could be spell effect rather than a contract action.
Memory loss, panic about an unpriceable blank, acquisition of people, and proprietary warmth were not visually recoverable.
At 96 pixels, the figure reduces to a conventional dark-coated woman with a book and extended hand.
preserved_uncertainties: The meaning of the skin script, book, and black liquid remains ambiguous.
Confidence, guarded appraisal, seduction, impatience, and neutrality remain competing unaided face reads.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Repeatedly assigned profitable identities; desire for acceptance without a fixed role; fear of permanent definition; adaptive self-presentation; theft of identity, chemistry, and trust; ingratiating vigilance immediately before theft.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads identified a side-scanning eye, crouched opportunistic approach, asymmetrical concealment, a knowing crooked smile, and a reaching hand already entering the viewer's space.
Those cues independently support target assessment, rapid adaptation to an opportunity, and imminent taking.
visual_discrepancies: The smile read as playful, sly, delighted, or appealing-rogue behavior rather than a tense plea to be liked.
The long amber hand read primarily as claws or a weapon, not specifically as a siphon stealing a valued trait.
Fear of being defined, longing for unconditional acceptance, and deliberate theft of identity were not visually isolated.
The pose read as a predatory lunge or stalk rather than clearly deferential bowing.
At 96 pixels, the bright claws dominate while the face, tail, species detail, and social strategy disappear.
preserved_uncertainties: Hunger, theft, curiosity, amusement, and planned violence remain competing unaided interpretations.
The amber material's biological, magical, or equipment status remains unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Choosing one future killed all others in memory; desire to keep every future as ammunition; fear of one irreversible aging timeline; hoarding and firing possible futures; ecstatic predatory delight in multiple deaths; ancient grief in conflict with feral pleasure.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads identified a crouched predatory attack, a body and face split into pale and black halves, narrowed target fixation, exposed teeth, and a huge membrane bow.
The mouth could include a grin, and the asymmetrical body visibly permits a reading of incompatible conditions along the firing axis.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face read resolved mainly as generic feral aggression; ancient grief, temporal pain, and simultaneous pleasure were not distinctly separated.
The mouth remained ambiguous among grin, snarl, and tension caused by the crossing cords.
Multiple futures, age displacement, irreversible choice, and the use of timelines as ammunition were not independently visible.
The cause of the pale-black split and the bow's biological or carried status remained uncertain.
At 96 pixels, the face, teeth, cords, and age-related detail collapse into the bow-and-body mass.
preserved_uncertainties: It remains unclear whether the viewer is prey or merely aligned with the weapon aim.
The firing mechanics and projectile remain unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Rejected after identifying marks were used against it; desire to approach without being named; fear of recognition; erasure of identifying traces and witnesses; vacant fixation on the weakest witness; longing to approach in conflict with refusal to be known.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified an erased mouthless face, black apertures, stitched and scarred skin, a slight head cant, low loaded limbs, and encircling hooked appendages.
The head tilt and forward crouch support alert attention and approach, while the absence of conventional expression supports a vacant nonhuman mechanism.
visual_discrepancies: The fixed apertures and absent mouth made curiosity, caution, dissociation, hunger, fear, submission, and predation visually interchangeable.
No unaided cue isolated the weakest witness as the target or showed recognition itself as the feared event.
Longing for contact, memory erasure, boundary rejection, and procedural witness extermination were not visually specific.
The surrounding arcs could be limbs, parasites, shadows, blades, or display elements rather than a clearly closing escape ring.
At 96 pixels, the attention mechanism disappears and the form becomes a pale center inside a dark radial swirl.
preserved_uncertainties: The appendages' attachment, agency, and combat function remain unresolved.
The emotional meaning of the head tilt remains unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Affection used to create an unpayable obligation; desire for a relationship beyond leverage; fear of unpriced care and debt; transactional control as survival; reciprocal obligation imposed on others; courteous welcome while measuring painful collection.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently centered the character on an oversized stamp, a long paper strip, administrative judgment, calculating attention, and a restrained asymmetric smile.
The eyes, head angle, hands, and weight all converge on the stamping action, visibly supporting a transactional or institutional mode of control.
visual_discrepancies: Without an affected person or visible consequence, the action read as clerk, tax collector, merchant, bureaucrat, or benign craft pride as readily as coercive branding.
The specified service smile versus clinical predatory measurement did not separate cleanly from a familiar sly goblin smirk.
The private wish for non-transactional affection and fear of being placed in debt were not visually available.
The prop could be a mundane official seal rather than a painful reciprocal-obligation weapon.
At 96 pixels, the stamp is a small dark knob and the unit reads first as a dark goblin holding pale paper.
preserved_uncertainties: Amusement, greed, vindictiveness, meticulousness, and satisfaction with completed work remain competing face reads.
The stamp's target, stakes, and effect remain unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: A pupil surpassed and left her after consuming her private ambitions; desire for permanent dependence called love; fear of being surpassed and made unnecessary; partial teaching and correction as survival; mentorship distorted into ownership; intimate approval while imposing a limit.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads identified a tall mature woman with direct controlled attention, an offered hand, a diagram-covered rectangular board, one raised brow, a restrained smile, and body-wide fissures.
The offered hand and socially directed face can accommodate intimate approval or controlled instruction, while the board can accommodate examination or correction.
visual_discrepancies: The unaided read ranged among recruitment, bargain, seduction, condescension, reassurance, and confidence; a favored-pupil relationship was not decisive.
Fear of being surpassed, engineered dependency, withheld truth, and genuine care in conflict with ownership were not visibly isolated.
The copper fissures did not alter muscle tension or movement and read more like surface pattern than inflamed nerves or an active psychological cost.
The board did not unambiguously read as a correction slate and the open hand did not unambiguously direct obedience.
At 96 pixels, the fissures and diagrams disappear and the figure becomes a dark-clad woman carrying a case or tablet.
preserved_uncertainties: The fissures may represent damage, stored power, tattoos, circuitry, or ornament.
The board's practical function and the offered hand's demand remain unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Cartel labor, debt, and public humiliation; desire for an impact that restores dignity; fear that complexity will expose helplessness; reduction of problems to three blows; other bodies made to absorb humiliation; controlled labor rage in conflict with desperate desire for release.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified a mature industrial-looking brawler, an enormously enlarged forward fist, a wide planted stance, immediate impact, facial tension, and a direct confrontational gaze.
The dominant fist and loaded body visibly support the specified focus on simple physical release through impact.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face read found a conventional tough-guy snarl or battle sneer rather than a specifically legible grievance or suppressed humiliation.
The specification says the lips stay compressed, while the sealed visual record observed a raised upper lip and partly exposed teeth.
The cheek's anticipated relief and the distinction between rigid restraint and desperate release were not cleanly visible.
Cartel debt, labor degradation, fear of complex problems, and the three-blow survival sequence were not recoverable from the image.
At 96 pixels, recognition depends on one pale fist and does not preserve facial psychology or the arm's anatomical-versus-armored construction.
preserved_uncertainties: Rage, enjoyment of dominance, pain-bracing, and direct challenge remain competing face reads.
Whether the arm is anatomy, armor, curse, or temporary transformation remains unresolved.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Exclusion after two depended-on people formed a direct bond; desire to make every relationship require her; fear that two parties will compare accounts; controlled translation and manufactured conflict; warm invitation split by surveillance of a second party; performed intimacy covering fear of exclusion.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified a wide split stance, one strong diagonal strand, an inviting open hand, a second low open hand, and a face turned toward off-frame action.
The opposition of two hands and the diagonal connection across the body can accommodate a two-vector mediation or control premise.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face record described one sideways eye direction, one slightly lifted brow, a faint smile, and relaxed cheeks; it did not identify two independently legible gaze pairs.
Warm invitation versus cool surveillance, fear of comparison, and exclusion from direct connection were not visible as a specific contradiction.
The face read as calm confidence, graceful performance, satisfaction, caution, curiosity, or concentration without visible hostility, compulsion, shame, or strain.
The strand could be a staff, ribbon, slash line, binding, cutting line, healing effect, or movement trace rather than a clearly physical refractor ligament.
At 96 pixels, eye direction, lattice, secondary strands, and the two-party psychological construction disappear.
preserved_uncertainties: The target and function of the strand remain unresolved.
The lattice may be consequence, body paint, scarring, or projected geometry.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: Irreversible bodily collapse postponed through stolen grafts; desire for continued existence without maintenance terror; fear of one unpaid moment of disintegration; debtor bodies incorporated as replacement material; circulatory hunger; fortress authority in conflict with panic about collapse.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified a huge low flesh-and-shell vault, exposed central and pincer hearts, invasive cords, rib cages, many supports, and an enormous clamp-like pincer.
Exposed vital organs, structural replacement-like anatomy, and the low many-legged fortress visually support bodily maintenance, vulnerability, hunger, and defensive authority.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed face crop found no oriented eye, mouth, head, or sensing structure, so attention, entitlement, and maintenance panic were not directly locatable.
A still image did not visibly establish the specified contracting central heart or show which vascular change carries current emotion.
Stolen debtor bodies, graft provenance, refusal to die, and the economic logic of unpaid maintenance were not independently visible.
The exact hierarchy among central heart, pincer organ, ribs, legs, and controlling anatomy remained unclear.
At 96 pixels, only a small reddish center remains from the exposed-organ system.
preserved_uncertainties: The head or controlling organ, sensing method, leg count, and pincer-heart function remain unresolved.
Heartbeat, limb posture, pincer movement, or an unseen organ could carry attention; the image does not decide among them.

per_unit_comparisons.specified_psychology: A grievance dismissed for lack of authority-approved evidence; desire for undeniable proof; fear that contradiction will erase the grievance and self built around it; feelings converted into evidence and people into confirming targets; cold moral satisfaction covering doubt.
visual_correspondences: The sealed reads independently identified a very long rifle, mature scarred face, narrowed sideways gaze, controlled mouth, practical ranged focus, and inscribed hanging strips.
The whole-body weapon line and steady attention can accommodate a proof-seeking target fixation.
visual_discrepancies: The sealed visual record read the character as a mature lean woman with an adult feminine face, while the specification identifies a nonbinary androgynous design.
The face read as generic cool competence, vigilance, or restraint rather than specifically cold moral satisfaction with a suppressed flicker of doubt.
The stock was not visibly shouldered and the eye did not align through the scope, so face and weapon did not fully agree as an active evidentiary shot.
The grievance, authority's demand for proof, fear of contradictory evidence, and reduction of people to citations were not visually recoverable.
The hanging strips could be decorative records rather than a mobile archive of witness proof.
At 96 pixels, the rifle remains but facial psychology, inscriptions, scars, and archive detail disappear.
preserved_uncertainties: Aiming, scanning, listening, posing, and pausing before a shot remain competing pose reads.
The strips' function, rifle technology, and relationship between weapon and supernatural identity remain unresolved.

uncertainties: The specification supplies internal history, motive, fear, and causal interpretation that a static character image cannot by itself confirm.

Several pose directions describe actions such as contraction, tracking a second party, swallowing an accusation, or closing an escape arc; the inspected evidence is static and does not establish motion.
The unit records contain no per-field source receipts inside E_PSYCHOLOGY_SPEC, so the provenance of individual authored psychology statements is not expressed within this file.
Age and sex-design labels are specification metadata; discrepancies above report only what the sealed images communicated unaided, not an independent identity claim.
